subject: [RFC PATCH 26/40] mm: page_alloc: refuse fragmenting fallback for callers with cheap fallback

2026-05-20.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's page allocation mechanism to refuse fragmentation fallbacks for certain callers, specifically those that have a cheap fallback path. The change is made to prevent early-boot failures in QEMU due to a coarse bail-out gate in the slowpath retry of get_page_from_freelist. A finer-grained refusal is introduced in __rmqueue, where it checks if the allocation can be absorbed by a tainted pageblock or free pages. If so, it returns NULL and increments the SPB_HIGHORDER_REFUSED counter. The patch also adds a new flag ALLOC_HIGHORDER_OPTIONAL to indicate callers with cheap fallback paths.
